title: CALENDAR FLAWS
excerpt:
Description
THIS IS A NEED. I have a dashboard that connects multiple boards together (we use the Calendar view) and I just need the calendar to be color coded by board BUT it randomly assigns colors that don’t go great …

posts.author: hollyvera (Holly Duque)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

THIS IS A NEED. I have a dashboard that connects multiple boards together (we use the Calendar view) and I just need the calendar to be color coded by board BUT it randomly assigns colors that don’t go great with our brand. I also think there is a flaw because when you select “One By One” “Color By” - it doesn’t actually allow me to color one by one (for example if for 1 board I want to color by Status but for another board I want to color just by the board…it won’t let me actually do that and it changes it for all). BIG FLAW!!

What are you trying to achieve

FIND A SOLUTION because this is a problem and makes no sense.

posts.author: kylecordes (Kyle Cordes)
posts.cookedHtml:
Your best bet here is that hopefully one of the numerous calendar add-ons in the App Marketplace has the extra configuration you need. If you get very lucky, some of the people that make those will see this and maybe one will point out that their product has the particular ability you’re looking for. Otherwise, you might have to make your way through trying a bunch of them. There appear to be dozens of calendar-related add-ons available.

monday.com

monday.com Marketplace

monday.com app marketplace, Expand the capabilities of your Work OS with monday apps

posts.author: zoe.sgt (Zoe | SGTLab)
posts.cookedHtml: I would also love this functionality to be made available. If there was a work around, that would be immensely helpful!

posts.author: JoseDaher (Jose Daher)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

You’re right. All widgets should have the same setup options, and the “Customize” option is the one missing.

The “Color by” option is too limited to work for your need.

Hopefully, they’ll get this fixed.

Best regards,

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @hollyvera,

Upvoted it for you!

There is one thing you could try:

  1. Add a status column to each of the boards whose items you want to colour
  2. Assign a fixed status with a colour of your choosing.
    image200×187 2.19 KB
  3. You can set the default item status using the default item values feature.
    image554×311 10.7 KB
  4. Optionally, hide the status column.
    image359×352 15.4 KB

This should solve two issues:

  • Allow you to pick a fixed colour for those boards.
  • Since all boards now have status columns, you can combine it with a “colour by status” for the other boards.

This is the result in Calendar Plus, but it works in monday’s own calendar just fine:

Lastly, if very specific brand colors are important, you can perform the same trick with the Color picker column type. This will only work with Calendar Plus, though, as it supports coloring by color picker, and mixing with different column types, like the status column.
